data.title: Bitcoin Sentiment Index Declines Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, CryptoQuant analyst Axel reported on social media that the Bitcoin Advanced Sentiment Index has dropped to approximately 46%, slightly below the neutral threshold of 50%. Earlier in June, market bullish sentiment surged above 80%, but the index has since declined. Despite a recent price rebound from $103,000 to $105,000, open interest and net buying volume have not provided effective support, with the volume difference remaining near zero.

Currently, Bitcoin's price is fluctuating between $103,000 and $105,000, with no significant signs of new buying interest. For the upward trend to continue, the index needs to rise back to the 60-65% range, which requires simultaneous growth in net active buying volume and open interest. Otherwise, the market may test the support level in the $102,000 to $103,000 range.

data.title: The 19-day continuous net inflow was ended, and the total net outflow of Ethereum spot ETF yesterday was US$2.176 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (June 13, Eastern Time), the total net outflow of Ethereum spot ETFs was US$2.176 million.
The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net inflow in a single day yesterday was Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH, with a single-day net inflow of US$6.6691 million. The current total net inflow of ETH has reached US$735 million.
The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net outflow in a single day yesterday was Fidelity ETF FETH, with a single-day net outflow of US$8.8451 million. The current total net inflow of FETH has reached US$1.594 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETF is US$10.03 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market value to the total market value of Ethereum) is 3.26%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$3.855 billion.

data.title: Iran says nuclear talks with US 'pointless'
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Iran said that dialogue with the United States on the nuclear program was "meaningless" after Israel launched its largest-ever military strike on Iran, and accused Washington of supporting the attack. "The actions of the other party (the United States) have made dialogue meaningless." Tasnim News Agency quoted Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Bagae as saying: "You can't ask for negotiations while allowing the Zionist regime (Israel) to attack Iranian territory." He said that Israel "successfully influenced" the diplomatic process and that Israel's attack would not have happened without Washington's permission. Iran earlier accused the United States of involvement in Israel's attack, but Washington denied the accusation and told Tehran at the UN Security Council that it was "wise" to negotiate on the nuclear program. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Ethereum Spot ETFs Experience Mixed Inflows and Outflows
data.description: According to PANews, data from SoSoValue indicates that on June 13, 2025, Ethereum spot ETFs experienced a total net outflow of $2.176 million. The Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH recorded the highest single-day net inflow of $6.6691 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $735 million. Conversely, the Fidelity ETF FETH saw the largest single-day net outflow of $8.8451 million, with its historical total net inflow reaching $1.594 billion. As of the latest update, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs stands at $10.03 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 3.26% compared to Ethereum's total market value. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $3.855 billion.

data.title: DRW Investments has bought nearly 4 million shares of Trump Media & Technology Group, worth $100 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the Financial Times, DRW Investments, an investment company founded by American financial tycoon Don Wilson, has bought nearly 4 million shares of Trump Media Technology Group, worth about $100 million, becoming one of the top investors in Trump Media Technology Group's cryptocurrency bets, but its competitor Jane Street is the largest investor in Trump Media Technology Group's cryptocurrency bets and has invested about $375 million. It is reported that DRW Investments is a major institutional player in the field of crypto assets and an investor in liquidity provider Cumberland. They hope that the Trump administration will reconsider the regulatory authority of the US securities industry. They have previously called for the establishment of a new regulatory agency to replace the US Securities and Exchange Commission and the US Commodity Futures Trading Commission. As of now, Trump Media Technology Group has not responded to DRW Investments' request for comment on the investment.

data.title: Ethereum Whale Wallets Increase Holdings Amid Retail Profit-Taking
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from crypto market analysis firm Santiment reveals that 6,392 wallets currently hold between 1,000 and 100,000 Ethereum (ETH). Over the past month, while retail investors have been taking profits, these 'whale' wallets have been rapidly increasing their holdings.

During this 30-day period, the group has net accumulated 1.49 million ETH, marking a 3.72% increase in their holdings. Presently, these wallets account for 26.98% of the total Ethereum supply.
